Chapter XLIJI—Glory of Indra's Banner
In the previous three chapters the author has treated
of agricultural and economic matters. Now he takes up for exposi-
tion political astrology. What I mean is that the auguries mention-
ed herein pertain to kings and their kingdoms.
•TigTT^ I
inn
Once the celestials submitted to the Creator :—"Oh Lord, we
are unable to withstand the demons in battle. Consequently, we
have come to you, the protector of the helpless."
^ i
zf ^ iRn
The Lord replied to the Gods thus :—''Lord Narayana repos-
ing on the Milky Ocean will grant you a banner, at sight of which
the demons will not be able to face you on the battlefield/'
